articles.context: The article reflects the ongoing political turmoil in Sudan following the 2019 ousting of President Omar al-Bashir. articles.context: The Revitalised Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan (R-ARCSS), signed in September 2018, aimed to end a civil war that erupted in 2013, leading to widespread violence and humanitarian crises. articles.context: تأتي هذه المقالة في وقت حساس للسودان، حيث تتواصل النزاعات المسلحة منذ اندلاع الحرب الأهلية في عام 2013، والتي أدت إلى تفاقم الأوضاع الإنسانية. في أبريل 2023، اندلعت اشتباكات جديدة بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع، مما زاد من معاناة المدنيين. بينما تسعى الأطراف المتنازعة إلى التوصل إلى اتفاق سلام، يبقى التساؤل حول كيفية معالجة آثار النزاع على الضحايا والمجتمعات المتضررة.

articles.title: ضغوط وتحركات مكثفة لحسم «الأمتار الأخيرة» من حرب السودان

articles.context: تأتي زيارة الفريق أول عبد الفتاح البرهان إلى السعودية وسلطنة عمان في سياق تصاعد التوترات في السودان بعد اندلاع النزاع المسلح بين الجيش وقوات الدعم السريع في أبريل 2023. هذه التحركات تأتي عقب مؤتمر برلين في سبتمبر 2023، حيث تم مناقشة سبل إنهاء الصراع، ويُعتقد أن ولي العهد السعودي محمد بن سلمان والرئيس المصري عبد الفتاح السيسي يسعيان لتعزيز الاستقرار في السودان.

articles.summary: The co-chairs of Ethiopia’s official creditors committee (OCC) report the country has yet to ink restructuring agreements with bondholders and commercial creditors that comply with its comparability of treatment (CoT) requirements. The committee, chaired by China and France, rejected an agreement in principle reached between the Ethiopian government and holders of its one billion dollar […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has been grappling with a severe debt crisis exacerbated by the Tigray conflict, which began in November 2020 and has strained the country's economy. The Ethiopian government has sought debt restructuring under the G20 Common Framework, but the lack of agreements with bondholders and commercial creditors, particularly after the OCC's rejection of a proposed deal in early 2023, highlights ongoing challenges in stabilizing the nation's financial situation.

articles.title: TPLF Central Committee Convenes in Axum as Administrative Rift Deepens
articles.summary: A high stakes meeting of the proscribed Tigray People Liberation Front (TPLF) Central Committee in Axum is intensifying fears of a political rupture in Tigray, with mounting indications that the party could move to reject the region’s interim administration and push to restore its pre-war government structure. The meeting, which began on April 15, 2026, […]
articles.context: The TPLF, once the dominant party in Ethiopia, has been embroiled in a brutal conflict with the federal government since November 2020, leading to a humanitarian crisis in Tigray. The November 2022 Pretoria Agreement aimed to cease hostilities, yet tensions remain high as the TPLF grapples with the legitimacy of the interim administration established in Tigray. The April 2026 meeting in Axum signals a potential shift towards rejecting this interim governance in favor of reinstating the TPLF's former authority.

articles.title: Education Ministry’s Digital Exam Scheme Triggers Parliamentary Uproar
articles.summary: The Education Ministry has come under intense parliamentary scrutiny over the effectiveness and readiness of its ambitious plan to conduct the upcoming university entrance exam entirely online, set to take place within the next few months. Earlier this year, the Minister of Education announced a landmark shift from paper-based exams to an entirely online modality […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's education system has faced significant challenges in recent years, particularly following the Tigray conflict that began in November 2020, which disrupted schooling and exacerbated existing inequalities. The government's push for a digital exam system, announced in early 2023, aims to modernize education but raises concerns about infrastructure and access, especially in rural areas. This scrutiny comes amid ongoing debates about governance and accountability in the wake of political reforms initiated by Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed.

articles.title: Illegal Fuel Trade Booms as Supply Disruptions Leave Drivers Idle
articles.summary: Drivers are paying exorbitant prices for fuel in a parallel market that has only grown more lucrative in the wake of the war in Iran and associated global supply disruptions. An anonymous source working as a driver for a private company told The Reporter that he encountered extreme fuel prices while operating in the Gambella […]
articles.context: The ongoing conflict in Ethiopia, particularly the Tigray War that began in November 2020, has severely disrupted supply chains and exacerbated fuel shortages. Additionally, the recent war in Iran has led to global supply disruptions, further straining resources in Ethiopia. The government's inability to stabilize fuel prices amid these crises has fueled a burgeoning parallel market, leaving drivers in regions like Gambella facing exorbitant costs and highlighting the broader economic instability in the country.

articles.title: Justice Can’t Wait: Why Dialogue and Accountability Must Go Hand in Hand
articles.summary: Ethiopia’s Ministry of Justice recently confirmed that the country’s transitional justice process will begin only after the conclusion of the ongoing national dialogue, which is scheduled to conclude in October 2026. At first glance, this sequencing may appear logical: dialogue first, justice later. Yet, this approach potentially undermines both processes. Transitional justice and national dialogue […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has been grappling with ethnic tensions and violence, particularly following the Tigray conflict that erupted in November 2020, resulting in widespread human rights abuses. The government's establishment of a national dialogue in 2022 aims to address these issues, but the delay in initiating transitional justice raises concerns about accountability for past atrocities. The dialogue is set to conclude in October 2026, leaving victims in limbo as justice remains contingent on political negotiations.

articles.title: No African Consensus on Transboundary Waters, Security: Ethiopia
articles.summary: AU requests AUDA-NEPAD for technical, financial support for Ethiopia’s COP32 preparations Ethiopia has raised its concerns regarding lack of African consensus on the nexus of climate change and security, particularly pertaining to transboundary waters and security. The AU has disclosed documents compiling the decisions, declarations and resolutions made during the 39th Assembly of the Union […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's concerns stem from ongoing tensions over the Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam (GERD), which has strained relations with downstream countries, particularly Egypt and Sudan. The 2015 Declaration of Principles aimed to foster cooperation but has faltered amid disputes over water rights and management. As climate change exacerbates water scarcity, Ethiopia's call for consensus highlights the urgent need for collaborative frameworks to address security and resource-sharing in the Horn of Africa.

articles.title: IMF Warns Iran Crisis could Stifle Sub-Saharan GDP Growth, Slashes Forecast
articles.summary: Fuel, food, fertilizer inflation rise could drive 20mln people into food insecurity A new forecast from the International Monetary Fund (IMF) warns that Sub-Saharan Africa could be hit hard by the global supply chain disruptions brought on by Washington’s war in Iran, with countries dependent on fuel imports especially vulnerable to the repercussions, while oil […]
articles.context: The ongoing conflict between Iran and the U.S. has escalated since the U.S. withdrew from the Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action (JCPOA) in 2018, leading to heightened tensions and sanctions that disrupt global oil supplies. Ethiopia, already grappling with internal conflicts and humanitarian crises, faces increased vulnerability as rising fuel and food prices threaten to exacerbate food insecurity for millions, particularly in the context of the Tigray conflict and ongoing drought conditions.

articles.title: Concerns over Political Influence in Civil Service Dominate GGA Forum Discussion
articles.summary: Concerns over political influence within Ethiopia’s civil service took center stage at a forum organized by the Good Governance Africa–Horn of Africa (GGA) in Addis Ababa, part of a series of three discussions focused on the upcoming national elections. The first session, held on April 16, 2026, under the theme “The Role of the Civil […]
articles.context: Ethiopia is currently navigating a complex political landscape marked by ethnic tensions and the aftermath of the Tigray War, which officially ended in November 2022 with a peace agreement. The upcoming national elections, scheduled for 2026, have heightened concerns regarding the impartiality of civil service institutions, particularly as various political factions, including the Prosperity Party and opposition groups, vie for influence amid ongoing unrest and calls for democratic reforms.

articles.title: Inflationary Pressure Builds Up as Hormuz Crisis Remains at Standstill
articles.summary: Ethiopia is projected to record inflation of 12.1 percent in 2026, placing it among a group of African economies facing renewed price pressures driven by rising global energy and food costs, according to a joint policy brief assessing the economic fallout of the Middle East conflict. The joint projection revealed by the African Development Bank, […]
articles.context: The ongoing Hormuz crisis, marked by heightened tensions following the U.S.-Iran conflict and disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, has exacerbated energy prices globally. Ethiopia, already grappling with economic challenges due to the Tigray conflict and the aftermath of the COVID-19 pandemic, faces additional inflationary pressures as it relies on imported energy and food. The African Development Bank's projection underscores the urgency for Ethiopia to stabilize its economy amidst these external shocks.

articles.title: Ministry Signals Rollout of Transitional Justice Policy with New Justice Bodies and Women’s Representation
articles.summary: The Ministry of Justice has signaled the rollout of Ethiopia’s transitional justice policy—one year after its adoption—outlining plans to establish key institutions and strengthen women’s representation in the process. The update was shared during a project closing and learning workshop organized by Timran, a CSO engaged in political empowerment, in Addis Ababa on April 14, 2026. The event marked the […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has been grappling with ethnic tensions and conflict since the Tigray War erupted in November 2020, leading to widespread human rights abuses. The transitional justice policy, adopted in April 2025, aims to address these grievances and promote reconciliation among affected communities. The establishment of new justice bodies and emphasis on women's representation is crucial in a context where women's voices have been marginalized in peace processes, particularly following the conflict's devastating impact on their lives.

articles.title: UN relief chief warns South Sudan nearing famine as crisis deepens
articles.summary: 'The world’s youngest nation stands at a dangerous crossroads,' Tom Fletcher tells Security Council

As of late 2023, over 7 million people are projected to face severe food insecurity, prompting urgent international attention.

articles.context: اندلعت الحرب في السودان في 15 أبريل 2023 بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع، مما أدى إلى أزمة إنسانية حادة. بعد ثلاث سنوات من الصراع، يسعى المجتمع الدولي، بما في ذلك ألمانيا، إلى إيجاد حل سياسي دائم، خاصة بعد فشل اتفاق جوبا للسلام في 2020 في تحقيق الاستقرار. تتزايد الضغوط على الأطراف المعنية، بما في ذلك الحكومة الانتقالية السابقة، لتقديم تنازلات من أجل إنهاء العنف.

articles.title: Z Library and the Global Spread of Educational Resources
articles.summary: Access to knowledge once moved at the pace of printed pages and locked doors. Libraries stood as quiet gates that opened only at certain hours. articles.context: In Ethiopia, the ongoing conflict in the Tigray region, which escalated in November 2020, has disrupted traditional educational systems and access to resources. Amidst this turmoil, digital platforms like Z Library have emerged as vital tools for students and educators seeking knowledge, circumventing barriers imposed by the conflict. The Ethiopian government's efforts to control information flow further underscore the significance of accessible digital libraries in promoting education and resilience in the face of adversity.

articles.title: حتى المحافظون الجدد انقلبوا ضد الحروب في الشرق الأوسط - مقال في الغارديان

This incident underscores the fragile political dynamics and ongoing disputes over territorial integrity in the Horn of Africa.

articles.title: نسخة منك في المستقبل ستتوسل إليك: لماذا لا نستمتع بلحظاتنا الحاضرة؟
